sections.paragraph:
αὐλέω 1 aor. ηὔλησα (Alcman, Hdt. et al.; SIG 1084–88 al.; StudPal XXII, 47, 5; Sb 7557, 5) play the flute τινί (X., Symp. 2, 8) for someone (to dance) Mt 11:17; Lk 7:32 (Aesop 27 H.=11 P., H-H. ὅτε ηὔλουν, οὐκ ὠρχεῖσθε; similarly 134 H.; Proverbia Aesopi 115 P.). τὸ αὐλούμενον what is played on the flute 1 Cor 14:7.—DELG s.v. αὐλός.

aor. aor. = aorist

Alcman Alcman , VII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

StudPal StudPal = CWessely, Studien zur Paläographie und Papyrusurkunden—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 150
